question: Did the review adhere to pre-defined objectives and eligibility criteria?
answers:
OBJECTIVE To estimate the treatment response of vitiligo to phototherapy.

All prospective studies reporting phototherapy outcome for at least 10 participants with generalized vitiligo were included

question: Were the eligibility crieria appropriate for the review question?
answers: All prospective studies reporting phototherapy outcome for at least 10  participants with generalized vitiligo were included.

question: Were eligibility criteria unambiguous?
answers:
participants of all age groups with a diagnosis of generalized or symmetrical vitiligo; (3) at least 1 phototherapy group, including NBUVB or PUVA; (4) at least 10 participants in each treatment arm, regardless of the dropout rate; (5) treatment duration of at least 12 weeks or at least 24 treatment sessions; (6) outcomes measured based on all vitiligo lesions on the participant's whole body or at least half of the body; and (7) outcomes measured according to the degree of repigmentation based on the quartile scale (>=25%, >=50%, and >=75%).

The outcome of interest was the repigmentation rate.

Protocol - Comparator(s)/control None. The review will take the form of a single-armed meta-analysis.

question: Were all restrictions in eligibility crieria based on study characteristics appropriate (e.g. date, sample size, study quality, outcomes measured)? If yes, indicate which study characteristic was an inclusion/exclusion criteria
answers:
4) at least 10 participants in each treatment arm, regardless of  the dropout rate; (5) treatment duration of at least 12 weeks or at least 24 treatment sessions; 

The types of phototherapy evaluated in this review were restricted to NBUVB and PUVA because other phototherapies have not been widely used for treatment of vitiligo. We also excluded targeted phototherapy, such as excimer laser and light, which are usually used to treat localized vitiligo. Combination therapies with any other intervention, such as topical agents, systemic corticosteroids, and antioxidants, were also not included.

question: Were any restrictions in eligibility criteria based on sources of info appropriate (e.g. publication status or format, language, availability of data)?
answers:
A comprehensive database search using predefined search terms (eTable 1 in the Supplement) was performed in MEDLINE, EMBASE, and the Cochrane library from inception to January 26, 2016.

All prospective studies were included with no language restriction

question: Did the search include an appropriate range of databases/ electronic sources for published and unpublished reports?
answers: A comprehensive database search using predefined search terms (eTable 1 in the Supplement) was performed in MEDLINE, EMBASE, and the Cochrane library from inception to January 26, 2016.

question: Were methods additional to database searching used to identify relevant reports?
answers: reference lists in relevant review articles were scanned manually.

question: Were the terms and structure of the search strategy likely to retrieve as many eligible studies as possible?
answers:
A comprehensive database search using predefined search terms (eTable 1 in the Supplement) was performed in MEDLINE, EMBASE, and the Cochrane library from inception to January 26, 2016. The main keywords used were vitiligo, phototherapy, psoralen, PUVA, ultraviolet, NBUVB, and narrow band. All prospective studies were included with no language restriction, and the reference lists in relevant review articles were scanned manually.

eTable 1. Predetermined Search Terms in Each Database

question: Were search strategy restrictions based on date, publication format, or language appropriate?
answers: A comprehensive database search using predefined search terms (eTable 1 in the Supplement) was performed in MEDLINE, EMBASE, and the Cochrane library from inception to January 26, 2016. The main keywords used were vitiligo, phototherapy, psoralen, PUVA, ultraviolet, NBUVB, and narrow band. All prospective studies were included with no language restriction

question: Were efforts made to minimise error in selection of studies?
answers:
All identified articles were screened independently by 2 reviewers (J.M.B. and H.M.J.).

Two reviewers (J.M.B. and H.M.J.) independently identified relevant articles by searching the titles and abstracts. If the abstract did not provide enough information to include or exclude the study, full-text evaluation was performed to determine eligibility. The reviewers compared the results, and discrepancies were resolved through discussion or, if necessary, by arbitration by a third reviewer (B.Y.H).

question: Were efforts made to minimise error in data collection?
answers: Data Extraction - The following information was extracted independently by 2 reviewers (J.M.B. and H.M.J.) from the eligible reports meeting the inclusion criteria: study design, number and characteristics of the participants, subtype and duration of vitiligo, type of phototherapy, initial dose, treatment frequency and duration, and numbers of participants with repigmentation based on the quartile scale.

question: Were sufficient study characteristics considered for both review authors and readers to be able to interpret the results?
answers:
Table 1. Characteristics of the Included Studies

eTable 2. Interventions and Clinical Outcomes of Phototherapy for Vitiligo

question: Were all relevant study results collected for use in the synthesis?
answers:
An intention-to-treat analysis was planned, and dropouts were included in the analysis, if possible. Otherwise, we included patients who were described in the final assessment.

Meta-analyses were performed separately according to the type of phototherapy (NBUVB and PUVA) and duration of treatment ( <=3, <=6, and <=12 months). We included oral and topical PUVA in the PUVA group in this review. We performed subgroup analyses to investigate the treatment response to NBUVB phototherapy by body site, categorized as (1) face and neck, (2) trunk, (3) extremities, and (4) hands and feet.

question: Was risk of bias (or methodological quality) formally assessed using appropriate criteria?
answers: Protocol - Risk of bias (quality) assessment - None planned.

question: Were efforts made to minimise error in risk of bias assessment?
answers: nan

question: Did the synthesis include all studies that it should?
answers:
Data Synthesis - Meta-analyses were performed separately according to the type of phototherapy (NBUVB and PUVA) and duration of treatment (<=3, <=6, and <=12 months). We included oral and topical PUVA in the PUVA group in this review.

Finally, 35  unique studies involving 1428 unique patients fulfilled the inclusion criteria for this review. Of these, 29 studies with 1201  patients were included in the NBUVB group (8-36) and 9 studies with 227 patients were included in the PUVA group1 (9,23,31,37-42) (eTable 2 in the Supplement).

eTable 2. Interventions and Clinical Outcomes of Phototherapy for Vitiligo

question: Were all pre-defined analyses reported or departures explained?
answers:
Protocol - Main outcome(s) - Repigmentation of 75%, 50%, and 25%, respectively. Measures of effect - Over 3 months of treatment duration. 

Strategy for data synthesis A single arm meta-analysis will be conducted, using methods reported previously.

Analysis of subgroups or subsets - Repigmentation rates according to body sites.

Figure 2. Treatment Response of Phototherapy for Vitiligo

Table 2. Summary of Findings for Phototherapy for Vitiligo

question: Was the synthesis appropriate given the nature and similarity in the research questions, study designs and outcomes across included studies?
answers: Single-arm meta-analyses were  performed for the NBUVB and PUVA groups. Sample size-weighted means were calculated using a random-effects model for the repigmentation rates of the included studies.

question: Was between-study variation (heterogeneity) minimal or addressed in the synthesis?
answers: Our systematic review had some limitations. First, the study design, characteristics of the enrolled patients, and phototherapy protocol had considerable heterogeneity.

question: Were the findings robust, e.g. as demonstrated through funnel plot or sensitivity analyses?
answers: nan

question: Were biases in primary studies minimal or addressed in the synthesis?
answers: Protocol - Risk of bias (quality) assessment -  None planned.

question: Did the interpretation of findings address all of the concerns identified in Domains 1 to 4?
answers: Our systematic review had some limitations. First, the study design, characteristics of the enrolled patients, and phototherapy protocol had considerable heterogeneity.

question: Was the relevance of identified studies to the review's research question appropriately considered?
answers: The present study demonstrated the treatment response of vitiligo to phototherapy according to phototherapy type, to reatment duration, and body site.

question: Did the reviewers avoid emphasizing results on the basis of their statistical significance?
answers: Yes
